titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD55/31

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=289 status=live modified=2021-07-20 11:22:27.420741+00 | translation moa=288 status=live modified=2021-04-06 10:04:06.456414+00

div.p:
Omnibus matris Ecclesie filiis presentibus et futuris . Rogerus de Scalebroc Salutem . Sciatis me concessisse et dedisse
et hac mea Carta confirmasse deo et Ecclesie sancte marie de Melr’ et monachis ibidem deo seruientibus pro animabus DauiD . et
Malcolmi Regum scotie et Comitis . Henrici . et pro anima Gilleberti filii Fergus domini mei . et uxoris eius . et pro animabus patris mei et
matris mee et omnium parentum meorum . et pro salute domini mei Regis Willelmi et Comitis . dauid . fratris eius . et Comitis . Dunecani et Du
necani filii Gilleb’ . domini mei et pro salute mea et omnium successorum meorum in liberam et puram et perpetuam Elemosinam totam ter
ram de Drumeteisuiene . et de Alesburc . et de Authnephur per has diuisas . Scilicet sicut una burna descendit de mussa
que est inter Largas et Bethoc . et diuidit inter Dunduf et Drumteisuiene et per eandem burnam usque ad illum locum ubi eadem burna
cadit in mare et inde per litus maris usque ad Alesburc . et de Alesburc usque ad sicum qui descendit de nemore ex orientali parte
uie quadrigarum ascendentis ad Authnephur . et per eundem sicum ascendendo per quandam profundam uallem usque ad quoddam uetus
Castellum . et ex orientali parte eiusdem ueteris Castelli per sicum sursum usque ad Tilliforde . ex aquilonali parte fontis . et inde sursum
in transuersum usque ad sicum qui uadit ex aquiloni parte de Farnileie . et inde sursum per sicum usque ad semitam . et per semitam sursum
usque ad capud sici qui cadit in polnesalahari ad Thauenegaith . Totam hanc terram et quicquid continetur infra prenominatas diui
sas in bosco et plano in pratis et pasturis . in moris et mariscis . in mineriis et salinis . et in omnibus aliis aisiamentis dedi pre
dictis monachis et Ecclesie de Melr’ tenenda et possidenda in perpetuum ita libere et quiete plenarie et honorifice soluta ab omni terre
no seruitio et consuetudine et exactione seculari  sicut aliqua elemosina liberius et quietius . plenius et honorificntius tenetur et possidetur a
monachis ordinis Cisterciensis . ita tamen quod dabunt mihi et heredibus meis unam marcam argenti tantum singulis annis ad recognitionem de
elemosina de Authnephur . dimidiam . scilicet marcam ad pentecosten . et dimidiam marcam ad festum sancti martini . et ipsi utentur omni
hoc tenemento pro libitu et uoluntate sua modis omnibus sicut melius et utilius sibi et suis uisum fuerit . Et quia uolui ut hec mea elemosi
na firma esset et stabilis et nullo modo posset in irritum duci . horum omnium testes et fideiussores posui Episcopum et Ecclesiam Glas
guensem . et Comitem . Dunecanum . et heredes eius . ut si aliquando ego uel heredes mei a tenore huius Carte deuiauerimus . ipsa Ecclesia et eius pontifici
ces per censuram ecclesiasticam ad corectionem nos reuocent . Hec autem omnia propria manu affidaui in manu domini Iocelini . Glasguensis . episcopi
pro me et pro heredibus meis sine malo ingenio tenenda et obseruanda . Et ego heredes mei manutenebimus et Warantizabimus
ipsis monachis eandem Elemosinam cum omnibus libertatibus et pertinentiis suis contra omnes homines . et adquietabimus de omni seruitio
erga dominos nostros in perpetuum annuente et testificante domino meo Dunecano . filio Gilleberti . Hiis testibus . Domino Iocelino . Glasguensi .
episcopo . Simone archidiacono . Hereberto Decano Glasg’ . Gillenem Accoueltan . Gilledoueng’ . fratre eius . Gillecrist mac makin .
Murdac mac Gillemartin . Gilleasald mac Gilleandris . Gillemernoch fratre eius .

To all sons of Mother Church present and future, Roger of Skelbrooke sends Greeting: Know that I have granted and given, and by this my charter made firm, to God and to the church of St Mary at Melrose and to the monks there serving God, for the souls of the kings of Scotland, David and Malcolm, and Earl Henry, and for the soul of GIlbert son of Fergus, my lord, and of his wife, and for the soul of my father and my mother and of all my ancestors, and for the salvation of my lord, King William, and of Earl David his brother, and of Earl Duncan, and Duncan son of Gilbert, my lord, and for my own salvation and of all my successors, in free and pure and perpetual alms, the whole land of Drumeteisuiene, and of Alesburc, and of Authnephur, by these boundaries, that is, as one burn descends from the moss that is between Largas and Beoch, and divides between Dunduff and Drumeteisuiene and by the same burn as far as the place where the same burn falls into the sea, and thence by the sea shore as far as Alesburc, and from Alesburc as far as the syke that descends from the wood on the eastern side of the wagon road that ascends to Authnephur, and by the same syke ascending by a deep valley as far as an old castle, and on the eastern side of the same old castle by the syke upwards as far as Tilliforde, from the northern side of the spring, and thence upwards and across as far as the syke which runs from the north side of Farnileie, and thence upwards by the syke as far as the path, and by the path upwards as far as the head of the syke that falls into the Polnesalahari to Thauenegaith; this whole land and whatsoever is contained within the aforenamed boundaries in wood and plain, in meadows and pastures, in moors and marshes, in mines and saltpans, and in all other easements I have given to the said monks and church of Melros, to be held and possessed in perpetuuity as freely and quietly, fully and honourably, loosed from all tenurial service and custom and secular exaction, just as any alms is freely and quietly, fully and honourably held and possessed by monks of the Cistercian Order, provided that they shall give me and my heirs one merk of silver only each year for recognition in respect of the alms of Authnephur, that is, half a merk at Pentecost, and half a merk at the feast of St Martin, and they shall make use of this tenement at their pleasure and will, by all means, as it might seem good and profitable to them and their men. And since I have willed that this my alms be firm and established and by no means able to be brought to nought, I have placed the bishop and church of Glasgow, and Earl Duncan and his heirs, as witnesses and sureties of all these things, so that if I or my heirs at any time deviate from the terms of this charter, this church and its bishops should recall us by ecclesiastical censure to correction. I have sworn all these things with my own hand in the hand of the lord Jocelin, bishop of Glasgow, for myself and for my heirs, without evil intention to be held and observed. And I and my heirs shall maintain and warrant this same alms to the monks against all men, with all its liberties and pertinents, and we shall discharge every service in respect of our lords in perpetuity, with my lord, Duncan son of Gilbert, assenting and bearing witness. With these witnesses, the lord Jocelin, bishop of Glasgow, Simon the archdeacon, Herbert the dean of Glasgow, Gilla na Náem Accoueltan, Gilledoueng’ his brother, Gilchrist mac Maken, Murdoch mac Gilla Martain, Gilla Asaild mac Gillanders, Gilla M’Ernóic his brother.
